title: luzula pediformis subsp. caespitosa
intro: Luzula pediformis subsp. caespitosa, also known as the tufted woodrush, is a subspecies of flowering plant that belongs to the family Juncaceae. It is a perennial plant that grows in dense tufts and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Luzula pediformis subsp. caespitosa include reducing inflammation, soothing wounds, and supporting the immune system. Luzula pediformis subsp. caespitosa can be used in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
